questions.question: What happens when you install an operating system on a new computer?
options: A) The computer's hardware is upgraded
B) The operating system is installed on top of the existing OS
C) The operating system is installed on the computer's firmware
D) The operating system is installed on the computer's hard drive
questions.explanation: The operating system is installed on the computer's hard drive, where it can be accessed and run by the computer's processor.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a 64-bit operating system?
options: A) It can only run 32-bit applications
B) It can run both 32-bit and 64-bit applications
C) It requires 32-bit hardware to run
D) It is slower than a 32-bit operating system
questions.explanation: A 64-bit operating system can run both 32-bit and 64-bit applications, providing greater flexibility and compatibility.

questions.question: A user is trying to install a new program on their Windows 10 computer, but they are getting an error message saying that the program requires administrative privileges. What should the user do?
options: A) Run the program as an administrator
B) Disable UAC (User Account Control) to avoid the error message
C) Uninstall the program and reinstall it as an administrator
D) Contact the program's developer for support
questions.explanation: The user should run the program as an administrator to gain the necessary privileges to install the program.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Task Manager in a Windows operating system?
options: A) To create new user accounts
B) To manage system processes and applications
C) To configure network settings
D) To update the operating system
questions.explanation: The Task Manager allows users to view and manage running processes and applications, as well as terminate or close them if necessary.

questions.question: A Linux user wants to set up a new user account with limited privileges. What command would they use to do this?
options: A) useradd newuser
B) passwd newuser
C) adduser newuser
D) usermod newuser -a -G limitedgroup
questions.explanation: The useradd command is used to create new user accounts in Linux, and it can be used to set up a new user account with limited privileges by adding them to a specific group.
